items.title: Worst diphtheria outbreak on record spurs $7m federal vaccine response

children.content: The federal government has stepped in to address the largest diphtheria outbreak since records began, unveiling a multi-million dollar package for more vaccines and workers to deliver them.

gallery.altText: A man in a navy suit and tie looks sternly into camera.

thumbnail.altText: A man in a navy suit and tie looks sternly into camera.

items.title: Australia's economy has 'already entered a downturn', economist confirms

image.altText: People walk across a street.

children.content: The unemployment rate rose by 0.2 percentage points to 4.5 per cent in April, up from 4.3 per cent in March, according to the Bureau of Statistics.

items.title: Australia backs landmark UN climate change ruling as others try to block it

children.content: Australia joins 140 other countries in passing a major United Nations resolution backing a landmark legal ruling on climate change, despite efforts by the United States, Russia and Saudi Arabia to sink it.

image.altText: A group of people stand together, raise their fists and hold signs calling for climate justice.

items.title: Musk's X ordered to pay $750,000 for not complying with Australian child protection request

children.content: Elon ​Musk's social media company X Corp has ​admitted ‌to not complying with an order from Australia's eSafety commissioner to ⁠provide information about the measures it has in place ​to stop ‌child exploitation on the X platform.

image.altText: Elon Musk pouts his lips in a close up portrait

items.title: Israeli minister's taunting of activists no surprise to those following his career

children.content: Benjamin Netanyahu has condemned a video of his national security minister taunting activists, including Australians, who have been detained. But it's the prime minister who allowed this behaviour to percolate.

articles.title: Israeli minister posts video taunting detained activists of Global Sumud Flotilla

children.content: Some of the detained activists were being pushed and dragged along the ground by Israeli forces while Itamar Ben-Gvir was taunting them.

image.altText: people kneeling on the ground as armed soldiers watch over

articles.title: Government calls for detained Australian flotilla members to be released from Israel

children.content: The Australian government says it is engaging with Israel over the welfare of 11 Australians detained, as Israeli forces fire "non-lethal" shots at two boats in a Gaza-bound aid flotilla.

image.altText: A woman sitting on a ship holding a sign calling for someone's freedom, with Palestinian flags flying in background.

gallery.altText: Ben-Gvir looks furious as he points at prisoners during a visit.

thumbnail.altText: Ben-Gvir looks furious as he points at prisoners during a visit.

avatar.alt: A man in a suit stands in a corridor of parliament house.

items.title: Results are out for the world's most powerful company, but investors aren't impressed

children.content: The world's most valuable company, tech giant Nvidia, has reported a record revenue but investors are not impressed with second-quarter sales forecasts.

image.altText: Nvidia logo and rising stock graph

items.title: Woman dies after being hit by police car in Cairns

children.content: A 71-year-old woman has died after being hit by a police car in Cairns this morning.

image.altText: A police car parked at an angle off a road with police tape in foreground

items.title: Arrow's MND diagnosis shines spotlight on debilitating condition that has no cure

children.content: As another rugby league player is diagnosed with motor neurone disease, we asked experts why so little is known about what causes it.

articles.title: NRL star Arrow reveals MND has already affected his speech

children.content: Jai Arrow reveals that MND has already affected his speech after announcing yesterday he was stepping away from the NRL after his shock diagnosis.

image.altText: Jai Arrow with his face in his hands at a sports press conference

gallery.altText: Jai Arrow at his retirement press conference

items.title: Singtel wants to sell minority stake in Optus, unemployment rate spikes

children.content: Optus owner Singtel says it is looking to sell a minority stake in it. And the jobless rate has jumped to 4.5 per cent. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: A white and blue Optus sign

items.title: Gen Zs who approach share market like 'lottery ticket' could pay more tax

children.content: Gen Zs who take a lottery ticket approach to investing in the stock market will likely pay more tax under the capital gains tax changes being proposed by the government.

image.altText: ASX

items.title: As Colbert wraps up The Late Show, late-night TV could be on shaky ground

children.content: Like other checks and balances on presidential power, late-night talk shows — once ratings juggernauts, woven into the fabric of American culture — are being stress-tested. Here's how they became a staging ground for the battle over free speech.

articles.title: Five reasons Stephen Colbert is one of the US's most important satirists

children.content: The best satirists do more than entertain. They influence public discourse and leave lasting marks on political life.

image.altText: Stephen Colbert sits behind a desk on the Late Show set

items.title: Why 70,000 people could decide future for 70 million Brits

items.title: Why Raúl Castro has been charged now over a plane downing 30 years ago

children.content: The United States has indicted former Cuban president Raúl Castro on murder charges stemming from 1996, reviving one of the lowest points in its tangled history with Cuba.

items.title: Government to slash 1,700 jobs as Abetz looks to rein in Tasmanian spending

children.content: The Tasmanian government will slash 1,700 jobs through natural attrition and negotiated voluntary redundancies, as Treasurer Eric Abetz unveils his second state budget.

image.altText: Treasurer Eric Abetz in front of a budget cuts sign and piles of money.

children.content: Arafura Rare Earths has announced its flagship project north of Alice Springs will go ahead, decades after it was first flagged as a prospect.

items.title: Peak body slams ministers' failure to attend Aboriginal housing talks

items.title: Snowtown killer James Vlassakis's image has been revealed. Here's why

children.content: The youngest of the Snowtown killers was the subject of a long-running suppression order that kept his face — or any description of his appearance — hidden for 25 years. Here's why that's been lifted.

items.title: Ed takes chance another one-in-500-year flood won't hit family home

children.content: It was six months before Ed Trotter could return home after record floods on the NSW Mid North Coast. A year on from the disaster, others are still without working kitchens and bathrooms.

items.title: Former NSW Young Australian of the Year dies aged 32

children.content: Nikhil Autar, who was diagnosed with leukaemia when he was 17 years old, was named NSW Young Australian of the Year in 2024.

items.title: Farm worker 'felt sorry' for pensioner who wanted his leg amputated, court hears

children.content: A jury hears a recording of John Yalu explaining to police that he panicked after he amputated the leg of a 66-year-old man he then realised was dying. The 41-year-old is standing trial for murder.

items.title: Rundown Rockhampton hotel sells for millions at court-ordered auction

children.content: After its closure in 2014, the formerly popular hotel became dilapidated and was frequented by squatters. It has now sold at a court-ordered auction for $5.75 million.

image.altText: Drone shot of multi storey concrete building with overgrown gardens, empty pool, smashed windows

children.content: As the birth rate hits a record low it's been revealed that almost 60 per cent of Australian parents are experiencing psychological distress, according to the largest-ever national parenting survey.

items.title: Iran examining US proposal as Trump says talks in 'final stages'

items.title: Can Australia make its own fertiliser without fossil fuels?

children.content: Most fertiliser is a by-product of the oil industry, which is why it's so vulnerable to the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. But there are other sources the industry could eventually switch to.

image.altText: Green crops in the foreground with machinery in the background.

items.title: El Niño looms as BOM releases long-range winter forecast

children.content: The Bureau of Meteorology is forecasting winter to be unusually warm across the entire country as an El Niño climate pattern is increasingly likely to take hold.

image.altText: A map of Australia showing a dry south, wetter north

items.title: The majority of Tuvalu has applied to relocate to Australia to escape climate change. What happens now?

children.content: Tuvalu is building new land to hold back rising sea levels, but the majority of its people are applying to relocate to Australia. As they do, many are confronting a disturbing question.
